name: claude-toolkit
description: BeSpunky's Claude Code skills, subagents and commands. Develop once here; install and continuously upgrade across all projects.

plugins.name: project-starter
plugins.description: Scaffold new BeSpunky-standard projects: integrated Nx monorepo + Angular (clean --minimal app, no demo) + devcontainer (Claude CLI & VS Code extension) + tailored CLAUDE.md. Generator-first.

plugins.name: engineering
plugins.description: BeSpunky engineering skills. Agnostic foundation (architect-mentality + architecture-first: solve through design, never a patch; fix the root cause; design + confirm refactors) plus technique domains with progressive-disclosure references: software-design, advanced-typescript, angular-architecture, angular-native-wrappers, nx-monorepo-and-dx.

plugins.name: browser-automation
plugins.description: Drive a real browser via Playwright (Chromium, pre-installed in BeSpunky devcontainers) — verify UI behavior end-to-end, reproduce bugs in the running app, capture before/after screenshots, scrape rendered DOM, generate test scaffolds via codegen. Loaded by default in scaffolded projects.

plugins.name: product-ux
plugins.description: BeSpunky product & UX design skills. keep-users-oriented: whenever you make someone wait or move them through a process, answer the three questions — what is the expected result, where am I, what is the next step — and pick the right feedback for deterministic (steps/progress) vs nondeterministic (estimate + notify) processes. A universal service-design principle, expressed primarily through software UI (loading/progress, async, multi-step flows, long-running jobs, notifications, optimistic UI). redesign-means-rethink: the anti-anchoring entry gate — when asked to REDESIGN a UI, treat it as a complete creative reconception FROM SCRATCH, never a modification or reskin of existing code; the existing implementation has ZERO design authority (do not read it to inform the new design), reconceive the form from intent/requirements/feeling while honoring the purpose (what it is for, its data and functionality), and read the old code only AFTER the new design exists to plan cleanup/overwrite/migration — then a redesign runs the trio from scratch. A targeted tweak is NOT a redesign. envision-the-experience: the upstream visioning move — imagine the world an interface lives in (its feeling, place, metaphor) before any layout or component, and design every element as a native inhabitant of that world; the screen is a place, not a page. Interrogate every element the spec names (a 'menu' might become a sunflower whose petals you pick), speak in sensory non-technical language that names no implementation, hold immersive back from busy (restraint over spectacle), and produce a Vision. These three form the experience-design trio (feeling → Staging → build), on one altitude ladder: sensory feeling → web-native art → engineering. stage-the-vision: the visual architect and creative MIDDLE — the Vision says what a screen should FEEL like but not what it should LOOK like, and grounding alone can't make ART (it only stops the generic/garish). This skill invents the bold, web-native artistic MOMENTS that turn the feeling into something you'd screenshot, staying at the ART level — it speaks the web's language (parallax, cinematic scroll, a character that turns to camera, a living background, type-as-image, reveal choreography) but describes what artful thing happens and how it feels, never how to build it (envision speaks pure feeling; stage speaks web-native art like 'a lion's face fills the hero and turns to meet your eyes as you scroll'; realize speaks engineering). Because a model isn't a native artist, it reaches art by inventing several BOLD concepts and choosing the striking-yet-true, STEALING from specific great work and adapting its moves, composing with craft (focal point, scale, negative space, the cinematic moment), and sourcing genuinely artful assets — grounded so it's not generic, restrained so it's not garish (bold != loud), and judged by an OUTSIDE EYE for beauty, never self-certified. Crucially it DECOMPOSES each moment to physical truth — light, shadow, material, colour, depth, form, texture as they really behave — and refuses the lone primitive that only symbolizes a phenomenon (warm light is never just a gradient; depth is never just a box-shadow). Produces the Staging (a bold concept + concrete described moments — each decomposed to physical truth and staged across form factors, mobile-first — + a visual system), confirmed at low fidelity before building (routing to references: the web as an art medium; inventing the concept; decompose to physical truth — the painter's physics; composition & cinematic staging incl. staging across form factors; stealing from great work; grounding, restraint & the taste gate). realize-the-vision: the craftsman that turns a Vision AND a confirmed Staging into a real, running interface by RESEARCHING the truest, soundest means before writing any code — surveys the field (GSAP, Motion, three.js/R3F/angular-three, Web Animations, CSS scroll-driven animations, View Transitions, Lottie/Rive, Canvas/SVG/WebGL, Web Audio, haptics) and its caveats (jank, layout thrash, reduced-motion, accessibility, mobile), knows when to build in code vs. when to source an asset (figurative/photoreal art is generated, licensed, or downloaded — never hand-coded into path-soup — with licensing, review, and art-direction), requires a confirmed Staging before building (else invokes stage-the-vision first), engineers each staged moment, never self-certifies aesthetics, designs and confirms the architecture before building (architecture-first), and verifies against the feeling and the Staging in the running app — routing to a reference library of technique clusters.
